evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 9 Questions but page shows ~17 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 4/10 — Has flashes of voice ('who's on aux') but mostly reads like SEO template output with SERP-style headings.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Some opinions exist (Duke's is touristy but fine, Pig and the Lady non-negotiable) but most claims are hedged generalities.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — A few fresh bits ('who's on aux,' 'group chat explodes') mixed with generic travel-blog cadence.
Point of view vs both-sides 6/10 — Takes stances in places but FAQ section drifts into neutral survey mode.
Quotable sentences 5/10 — A couple of lines have snap ('who's on aux'), but most sentences are functional not memorable.

specificity 6/10 — The budget section introduces a cost table but the surrounding prose retreats into abstractions — 'costs can spiral,' 'split smart,' 'saving hundreds.' The table itself is specific, but the framing copy fails to anchor the advice in a concrete scenario or named comparison. The sentence flagged floats completely free of any number, property name, or dollar figure, undermining the otherwise data-rich post.
